about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: And, of course, Norman was the bass player. I think he's out in Frisco now... Fred Norman was a trombone player with Tommy Miles, too. He was in Tommy Miles' band even before Trummy Young

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: in New York there were quite a few guys during that era, like there was a great arranger named Fred Norman. He was in Claude Hopkins' band, and he was a trombonist and he wrote real good

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Fred Norman was a guy -- Fred Norman's been around as long as I can remember trying to get a job as an arranger, and he's one of the finest arrangers there is.

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: Fred Norman made the beautiful arrangement. That's how I was introduced in Claude's band. He was very kind to me.
